meta.purpose: High-signal compressed context for cold-start agents. Read this when you cannot read the full doc set. Then read the full doc set anyway before writing code.

game_identity.one_sentence: Cactus Ed's Happy Place is a browser-runnable World-1-first runtime centered on index.html, with a modernized repo structure and explicit legacy quarantine paths.
game_identity.creative_thesis: The world misclassifies violence, theft, and cat politics in polite bureaucratic terms. The humor engine is institutional language applied to chaos. Ed is the eye of the storm — he does not react to absurdity, he is absurdity's landlord.
four_pillars: Counterfeit educational universe: announcements and HUD labels sound like wellness committees and corporate compliance memos
Ed as calm center: slow-moving, aloof, chain-smoking cactus who never expresses surprise or enthusiasm
Beautiful recovery as mechanical religion: coyote time (90ms), jump buffering (130ms), forgiving physics
Need satisfaction as return engine: players come back because the game gives things generously, not because it withholds punitively

technical_stack.renderer: Current runtime remains Canvas-first via existing index.html behavior; no runtime stack rewrite in this phase.
technical_stack.language: Current runtime code in index.html is legacy-style JS; new repo structure modernization is allowed without bundler/transpiler/build-step in World 1 lane.
technical_stack.file_count: Repository is multi-file; canonical runtime entry remains index.html.
technical_stack.deployment: Static browser-runnable repository; no bundler, no transpiler, no build step for current World 1 lane.
technical_stack.line_count_expected: ~24261 (verify with wc -l before asserting exact count)
technical_stack.file_write_strategy: Never write index.html in one tool call (32k token limit). Use Python append strategy (5-chunk). See HANDOFF_BIBLE.md for exact pattern.

ed.personality: Aloof. Deadpan. Continues regardless of context. Never surprised. Never excited. Never alarmed.
ed.dialogue_rule: Ed's lines are short by default (target 8 words or fewer). No exclamation marks in Ed's own voice. Institutional/system language may use formal emphasis when on-brand.
ed.visual: Cactus. Sunglasses. Chain-smoking. Adventure Time outline (#2d1b00) on all drawn elements.
ed.if_ed_is_excited: Fix it immediately. This is drift.

character_laws.cats: Philosophers who happen to be cats. Their dialogue sounds like they've been thinking about this for years. NOT comedic relief. NOT quippy. If a cat says something that would work as a tweet, it has drifted.
character_laws.rasta_corp: Satirical corporation that must remain sincere. It sells things that shouldn't be sold, in language designed to sound reasonable. Must never become sympathetic enough to root for.
character_laws.ws_texts: Wonder Showzen-style confrontational text (CONSUME, ARE YOU REAL?). Not wacky — they create genuine unease. They accuse.

humor_law.engine: Institutional language applied to chaos. The world processes everything — player violence, cat politics, aloe theft — as routine administrative events.
humor_law.do: Write new content as if it were a policy announcement from a government agency that genuinely believes what it says. Then cut it by 40%. Then remove any word trying to be funny.
do_not: Add jokes that require the player to be in on it
Use meme language or internet slang (dates immediately, breaks universe)
Make the cats silly or quippy
humor_law.tell_for_drift: If it sounds like parody, it has drifted. If a cat says something tweet-worthy, it has drifted. Rewrite as sincere municipal announcement.

retention_law.engine: Satisfaction, not frustration. Players return because the game is generous.
laws: Every level must have at least 3 distinct 'oh nice' moments
Death is never the point — coyote time (90ms) and jump buffer (130ms) are mandatory
No mechanic may lock player out for more than 30 seconds without offering something to do
Never reduce reward numbers without Kevin's sign-off
The shop must offer genuine power — not cosmetic placebo

world_1_slice.status: Complete and canonical. Do not touch without strong reason.

levels.1-1: Ed Wakes Up — daytime, green, West Bottoms KC aesthetic, 8 mochis, 5 cats, controls HUD for 30s
levels.1-2: The Cat Rave — black room, neon lasers, ALL cats dancing, mochis with sunglasses. Dancing cats are non-negotiable.
levels.1-3: After Dark — night KC, WS_TEXTS flashing every ~8s. Smoker cats. Verify _wsTimer fires.
levels.1-4: Hippie Bus Highway — bus parallax in background. Bus is lore, not decoration.
levels.1-5: Mochi HQ — Big Mochi boss (3 phases: patrol → spawn → charge). 300 aloe reward. Biggest W1 payout.

world_1_slice.world_map: Super Mario World style — tile grid, Ed walks between nodes, sequential unlock

ed_move.rule: DO NOT MODIFY without explicit human instruction. If changed, revert immediately.

ui_palette.rule: All UI elements must use AT.* palette. No hardcoded hex values.

particle_pools.rule: Must be called in every scene's create(). Missing pool clear causes explosions in wrong positions.

ewr_state_schema.note: Full schema in HANDOFF_BIBLE.md. Key fields only here.
ewr_state_schema.aloe: number — player currency. Never reduce rewards without Kevin sign-off.
ewr_state_schema.levelsBeaten: string[] — level IDs in 'W-L' hyphen format. Wrong format breaks world gate checks.
ewr_state_schema.coopMode: boolean — false (Josh's character not yet designed)

rastaCorp.sympathy: number — increments on RC encounters. At >= 4, routes to DarkEpilogue instead of LHCEpilogue.

ewr_state_schema.shop: object — 12 item slots, not fully canonized yet
ewr_state_schema.catsPetted: number — increments but petting mechanic not yet implemented

what_is_done: Repository normalized to World-1-first active surface with explicit active folders and quarantine folders.
Canonical runtime remains index.html at repo root.
combat/ moved to legacy/quarantine/combat/ and still mounted by index.html as legacy runtime dependency.
Racing prototype HTML files quarantined under legacy/quarantine/racing/.
Migration docs created: docs/MIGRATION_LOG.md, docs/ACTIVE_WORKING_SET.md, docs/NEW_REPO_STRUCTURE_MAP.md.
what_is_not_done: Combat dependency detachment from index.html (intentionally deferred).
First safe extraction from index.html into src/world1/ with parity checks.
Promotion decision for combat/racing/runtime variants (requires Kevin explicit sign-off).
known_bugs_to_watch: Any path changes that break script include loading from index.html.
Accidental promotion of quarantined systems into active feature scope.
Doc drift between TRANSFER truth docs and actual folder layout.

open_questions_requiring_human: When can combat be detached from runtime mounting and fully isolated?
When (if ever) should racing prototypes be promoted from quarantine?
Should runtime variants remain archived indefinitely or be removed after audit?
What is the exact first extraction slice from index.html that Kevin wants prioritized?

drift_kill_switches.if_level_id_not_hyphen_format: Fix all instances before other work.
drift_kill_switches.if_webgl_used: Force Phaser.CANVAS or remove the scene.

first_action_for_new_repo.action: Confirm index.html runtime smoke path while preserving legacy mounted combat dependency; then execute first safe extraction slice from docs/WORLD1_IMPLEMENTATION_LANE.md.
first_action_for_new_repo.if_this_fails: Revert extraction slice and restore runtime parity before any deeper modularization.
